content:
Based on previous discussions, I can make some assumptions about what you want to fix in your code. First, you want to display 7 days of data in the "Daily" graph and 4 weeks of data in the "Weekly" graph. Your current code doesn't seem to limit the number of days or weeks in the graph to meet these requirements.

Let's analyze the code and make the necessary changes. I will provide solutions for both "Daily" and "Weekly" graphs.

1. In `fetchExpensesData`, after you set the start and end calendar for each time frame, you need to use these calendars in your Firestore query to fetch only the relevant data.

Here's how to modify your `"Daily"` and `"Weekly"` cases to fetch the data between start and end dates:

```kotlin
when (timeFrame) {
    "Daily" -> {
        // Daily: Showing 7 days in a week
        ...
        
        // Add this at the end of the "Daily" case
        query = query.whereGreaterThanOrEqualTo("date", startCalendar.time)
            .whereLessThanOrEqualTo("date", endCalendar.time)
        ...
    }

    "Weekly" -> {
        // Weekly: Showing 4 weeks in a month
        ...
        
        // Add this at the end of the "Weekly" case
        query = query.whereGreaterThanOrEqualTo("date", startCalendar.time)
            .whereLessThanOrEqualTo("date", endCalendar.time)
        ...
    }
    ...
}
```

Replace `"date"` with the appropriate field name in your Firestore documents that stores the date.

Now, the query will only fetch the relevant data between the start and end dates for each time frame.

2. Make sure the xAxisLabels are correct for the time frame you're fetching.

In the `"Daily"` and `"Weekly"` cases, you're currently using the day of the week and week number as xAxisLabels, respectively. You should update the xAxisLabels according to the specific date you're showing on the graph.

For example, let's update the xAxisLabels for the `"Daily"` graph to display specific dates within the 7 days. Replace the line that updates `xAxisLabels.value` in the `"Daily"` case with the following code:

```kotlin
val c = Calendar.getInstance()
c.time = startCalendar.time
xAxisLabels.value = mutableListOf<String>()
while (c.timeInMillis < endCalendar.timeInMillis) {
    xAxisLabels.value.add(dateFormat.format(c.time))
    c.add(Calendar.DATE, 1)
}
```

This will update the xAxisLabels to show specific dates for 7 days (like "Mon_21_Sep_2021").

You can do a similar update for the xAxisLabels in the `"Weekly"` case. However, it might be better to display the week number on the xAxisLabels, as you're already doing. You can even improve it by adding the month name to each week, like "Week 1 Sep" or "Week 2 Sep".

These changes should help you display 7 days for the "Daily" graph and 4 weeks for the "Weekly" graph. Please let me know if you have any questions or need further assistance.

content: Smart cast to 'MutableList<String>' is impossible, because 'xAxisLabels.value' is a complex expression

My apologies for the confusion. You're right; directly assigning a new instance to `xAxisLabels.value` using a complex expression might not work. Instead, you can create a new list first, populate it, and then assign it to `xAxisLabels.value`.

Replace the code that updates `xAxisLabels.value` in the "Daily" case with the following:

```kotlin
val c = Calendar.getInstance()
c.time = startCalendar.time
val newLabels = mutableListOf<String>()
while (c.timeInMillis < endCalendar.timeInMillis) {
    newLabels.add(dateFormat.format(c.time))
    c.add(Calendar.DATE, 1)
}
xAxisLabels.value = newLabels
```

This code first creates a new list called `newLabels`, populates it with the specific dates within the 7 days, and then assigns `newLabels` to `xAxisLabels.value`.

Similarly, if you want to update the xAxisLabels for the "Weekly" graph, you can follow the same approach with a new list, populate it accordingly, and then assign it to `xAxisLabels.value`.

content: now it is showing 10 days for the daily graph and sunday is missing

It seems like the number of days shown in the "Daily" graph is not correct. Let's fix the issue by making sure the start and end calendar values are correct and that the xAxisLabels are also being updated correctly.

To fix the issue with the "Daily" graph, first, update the start and end calendar values in the "Daily" case to fix their week starting and ending days. Here's the updated code:

```kotlin
"Daily" -> {
    // Daily: Showing 7 days in a week
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_WEEK, startCalendar.firstDayOfWeek)
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)

    endCalendar.time = startCalendar.time
    endCalendar.add(Calendar.DATE, 6)
    endCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
    endCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)

    ...
```

Now, update the code that sets xAxisLabels for the "Daily" graph to make sure it updates with the correct values:

```kotlin
val c = Calendar.getInstance()
c.time = startCalendar.time
val newLabels = mutableListOf<String>()
while (c.timeInMillis <= endCalendar.timeInMillis) {
    newLabels.add(dateFormat.format(c.time))
    c.add(Calendar.DATE, 1)
}
xAxisLabels.value = newLabels
```

Note the change in the while loop condition from `<` to `<=`. This will ensure that the loop iterates for all 7 days, including Sunday.

With these changes, the "Daily" graph should now show 7 days, including Sunday. Remember to apply necessary changes for the "Weekly" graph as well if required. Let me know if there are any other issues or further assistance is needed.

content:
fun fetchExpensesData(timeFrame: String) {
        val startCalendar = Calendar.getInstance()
        val endCalendar = Calendar.getInstance()
        var dateFormat = SimpleDateFormat("EEEE_dd_MMM_yyyy", Locale.getDefault())

        val query = db.collection("expenses")
            .document("Kantin")
            .collection(timeFrame.lowercase())

        query.get().addOnSuccessListener { result ->
            val expenses = mutableListOf<Pair<Float, Date>>()
            for (document in result) {
                val total = document.getDouble("total")?.toFloat() ?: 0f
                val date = dateFormat.parse(document.id) ?: Date()
                expenses.add(total to date)
            }

            when (timeFrame) {
                "Daily" -> {
                    // Daily: Showing 7 days in a week
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_WEEK, startCalendar.firstDayOfWeek)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)

//                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_WEEK, startCalendar.firstDayOfWeek + 2)
                    endCalendar.add(Calendar.DATE, 6)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
                    dateFormat = SimpleDateFormat("EEEE_dd_MMM_yyyy", Locale.getDefault())
                    
                    val c = Calendar.getInstance()
                    c.time = startCalendar.time
                    val newLabels = mutableListOf<String>()
                    println(c.timeInMillis)
                    println(endCalendar.timeInMillis)
                    while (c.timeInMillis <= endCalendar.timeInMillis) {
                        newLabels.add(dateFormat.format(c.time))
                        c.add(Calendar.DATE, 1)
                    }
                    xAxisLabels.value = newLabels

                    bottomAxisValueFormatter =
                        AxisValueFormatter { x, _ -> xAxisLabels.value[x.toInt() % xAxisLabels.value.size] }

                    println("1" + startCalendar.time)
                    println("2" + endCalendar.time)
                    val startDocId = dateFormat.format(startCalendar.time)
                    val endDocId = dateFormat.format(endCalendar.time)
                    query.orderBy(FieldPath.documentId()).startAt(startDocId).endAt(endDocId)
                }

                "Weekly" -> {
                    // Weekly: Showing 4 weeks in a month
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH, 1)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(
                        Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH,
                        startCalendar.getActualMaximum(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH),
                    )
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
                    dateFormat = SimpleDateFormat("W_MMM_yyyy", Locale.getDefault())

                    xAxisLabels.value = (1..4).map { "Week $it" }
                    bottomAxisValueFormatter =
                        AxisValueFormatter { x, _ -> xAxisLabels.value[x.toInt() % xAxisLabels.value.size] }
                }

                "Monthly" -> {
                    // Monthly: Showing 12 months in a year
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MONTH, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH, 1)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MONTH, 11)
                    endCalendar.set(
                        Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH,
                        endCalendar.getActualMaximum(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH)
                    )
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
                    dateFormat = SimpleDateFormat("MMM_yyyy", Locale.getDefault())

                    xAxisLabels.value = listOf(
                        "Jan",
                        "Feb",
                        "Mar",
                        "Apr",
                        "May",
                        "June",
                        "July",
                        "Aug",
                        "Sep",
                        "Oct",
                        "Nov",
                        "Dec"
                    )
                    bottomAxisValueFormatter =
                        AxisValueFormatter { x, _ -> xAxisLabels.value[x.toInt() % xAxisLabels.value.size] }
                }

                "Yearly" -> {
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MONTH, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH, -1)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MONTH, 11)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH, 31)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
                    dateFormat = SimpleDateFormat("yyyy", Locale.getDefault())
                }

                else -> {
                    throw IllegalArgumentException("Unsupported time frame: $timeFrame")
                }
            }

            val expensesMap = mutableMapOf<String, Float>().apply {
                xAxisLabels.value.forEach { put(it, 0f) }
            }

            expenses.groupBy({ dateFormat.format(it.second) }, { it.first })
                .forEach { date, values ->
                    values.sum().let { expenseSum ->
                        expensesMap[date] = expensesMap.getOrDefault(date, 0f) + expenseSum
                    }
                }

            for (expense in expenses) {
                val dateFormatted = dateFormat.format(expense.second)
                expensesMap[dateFormatted] = expense.first
            }

            chartEntries.value =
                entriesOf(*expensesMap.entries.mapIndexed { index, (_, expense) -> index.toFloat() to expense }
                    .toTypedArray())
        }
    }

content:
fun fetchExpensesData(timeFrame: String) {
        val startCalendar = Calendar.getInstance()
        val endCalendar = Calendar.getInstance()
        var dateFormat = SimpleDateFormat("EEEE_dd_MMM_yyyy", Locale.getDefault())

        startCalendar.firstDayOfWeek = Calendar.MONDAY
        endCalendar.firstDayOfWeek = Calendar.MONDAY

        val query = db.collection("expenses")
            .document("Kantin")
            .collection(timeFrame.lowercase())

        query.get().addOnSuccessListener { result ->
            val expenses = mutableListOf<Pair<Float, Date>>()
            for (document in result) {
                val total = document.getDouble("total")?.toFloat() ?: 0f
                val date = dateFormat.parse(document.id) ?: Date()
                if (date.time >= startCalendar.timeInMillis && date.time <= endCalendar.timeInMillis) {
                    expenses.add(total to date)
                }
            }

            when (timeFrame) {
                "Daily" -> {
                    // Daily: Showing 7 days in a week
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_WEEK, startCalendar.firstDayOfWeek)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)

//                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_WEEK, startCalendar.firstDayOfWeek + 2)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_WEEK, startCalendar.firstDayOfWeek + 6)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 23)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 59)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 59)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 999)
                    dateFormat = SimpleDateFormat("EEEE_dd_MMM_yyyy", Locale.getDefault())

                    val c = Calendar.getInstance()
                    c.time = startCalendar.time
                    val newLabels = mutableListOf<String>()
                    println(c.timeInMillis)
                    println(endCalendar.timeInMillis)
                    while (c.timeInMillis <= endCalendar.timeInMillis) {
                        newLabels.add(dateFormat.format(c.time))
                        c.add(Calendar.DATE, 1)
                    }
                    xAxisLabels.value = newLabels

                    bottomAxisValueFormatter =
                        AxisValueFormatter { x, _ -> xAxisLabels.value[x.toInt() % xAxisLabels.value.size] }

                    println("1" + startCalendar.time)
                    println("2" + endCalendar.time)
                    val startDocId = dateFormat.format(startCalendar.time)
                    val endDocId = dateFormat.format(endCalendar.time)
                    query.orderBy(FieldPath.documentId()).startAt(startDocId).endAt(endDocId)
                }

                "Weekly" -> {
                    // Weekly: Showing 4 weeks in a month
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH, 1)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(
                        Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH,
                        startCalendar.getActualMaximum(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH),
                    )
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
                    dateFormat = SimpleDateFormat("W_MMM_yyyy", Locale.getDefault())

                    xAxisLabels.value = (1..4).map { "Week $it" }
                    bottomAxisValueFormatter =
                        AxisValueFormatter { x, _ -> xAxisLabels.value[x.toInt() % xAxisLabels.value.size] }
                }

                "Monthly" -> {
                    // Monthly: Showing 12 months in a year
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MONTH, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH, 1)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MONTH, 11)
                    endCalendar.set(
                        Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH,
                        endCalendar.getActualMaximum(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH)
                    )
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
                    dateFormat = SimpleDateFormat("MMM_yyyy", Locale.getDefault())

                    xAxisLabels.value = listOf(
                        "Jan",
                        "Feb",
                        "Mar",
                        "Apr",
                        "May",
                        "June",
                        "July",
                        "Aug",
                        "Sep",
                        "Oct",
                        "Nov",
                        "Dec"
                    )
                    bottomAxisValueFormatter =
                        AxisValueFormatter { x, _ -> xAxisLabels.value[x.toInt() % xAxisLabels.value.size] }
                }

                "Yearly" -> {
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MONTH, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH, -1)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MONTH, 11)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH, 31)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
                    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
                    dateFormat = SimpleDateFormat("yyyy", Locale.getDefault())
                }

                else -> {
                    throw IllegalArgumentException("Unsupported time frame: $timeFrame")
                }
            }

            val expensesMap = mutableMapOf<String, Float>().apply {
                xAxisLabels.value.forEach { put(it, 0f) }
            }

            expenses.groupBy({ dateFormat.format(it.second) }, { it.first })
                .forEach { date, values ->
                    values.sum().let { expenseSum ->
                        expensesMap[date] = expensesMap.getOrDefault(date, 0f) + expenseSum
                    }
                }

            for (expense in expenses) {
                val dateFormatted = dateFormat.format(expense.second)
                expensesMap[dateFormatted] = expense.first
            }

            chartEntries.value =
                entriesOf(*expensesMap.entries.mapIndexed { index, (_, expense) -> index.toFloat() to expense }
                    .toTypedArray())
        }
    }

I'm glad the daily graph works as expected. To fix the "Weekly" timeframe, we need to adjust some logic inside the "Weekly" case.

First, update the start and end calendar values in the "Weekly" case to match the start and end weeks of the current month. You can compute the start and end of the weeks by getting the first week of the month and adding three weeks to it.

Here's the updated code inside the "Weekly" case for setting the start and end calendar values:

```kotlin
"Weekly" -> {
    // Weekly: Showing 4 weeks in a month
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH, 1)
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)

    // Move to the first day of the week
    while (startCalendar.get(Calendar.DAY_OF_WEEK) != startCalendar.firstDayOfWeek) {
        startCalendar.add(Calendar.DATE, -1)
    }

    endCalendar.time = startCalendar.time
    endCalendar.add(Calendar.DATE, 3 * 7) // Move to the end of the fourth week
    endCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 23)
    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 59)
    endCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 59)
    endCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 999)
    
    dateFormat = SimpleDateFormat("W_MMM_yyyy", Locale.getDefault())

    val startDocId = dateFormat.format(startCalendar.time)
    val endDocId = dateFormat.format(endCalendar.time)
    query = query.orderBy(FieldPath.documentId()).startAt(startDocId).endAt(endDocId)

    ...
}
```

This will compute the correct start and end calendar values for the "Weekly" timeframe at the beginning of the fourth week.

Also, make sure you update the logic to create xAxisLabels for the "Weekly" graph if necessary to reflect the correct labels for the four weeks.

With these changes, your weekly graph should now show only the four weeks of the current month. Let me know if there are any other issues or if you need further assistance.

content:
Understood, you want to display the first 4 weeks of each month in the weekly timeframe. 

To implement this, use the startCalendar to point to the first weekday of the first week of the month, then update the endCalendar to point to the last weekday of the fourth week of that month. Here's how to update the "Weekly" case:

```kotlin
"Weekly" -> {
    // Weekly: Showing 4 weeks in a month
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH, 1) // Set to the first day of the month
    while (startCalendar.get(Calendar.DAY_OF_WEEK) != startCalendar.firstDayOfWeek) {
        startCalendar.add(Calendar.DATE, -1) // Move backward until reaching the first day of the week
    }
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 0)
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MINUTE, 0)
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
    startCalendar.set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)

    endCalendar.time = startCalendar.time
    endCalendar.add(Calendar.DATE, 4 * 7 - 1) // Move forward 4 weeks (minus 1 day to include the start day)
    endCalendar.set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, 23)
    end
